design_philosophy: Tactical Minimalism. The map is the canvas, UI is the floating control layer. Google Maps-style clarity meets modern premium web design. Extremely fast, zero-clutter, thumb-zone optimized.

rules: Metrics and ETA must use tabular-nums for non-jittery updates.
Avoid centering text in cards; left-align for quick scanning.

layout_and_spacing.bottom_zone: Primary interactions happen in the bottom 20% of the screen (thumb-zone).
layout_and_spacing.border_radius: rounded-2xl or rounded-3xl for floating cards

Map.instructions: Full viewport. Disable zoom controls on mobile, rely on pinch. Map center: Anantapur, AP, India [14.6819, 77.6006].

FloatingActionCard.classes: bg-white rounded-3xl shadow-[0_20px_40px_-15px_rgba(0,0,0,0.15)] border border-slate-100 p-6 absolute bottom-6 left-6 right-6 md:left-1/2 md:-translate-x-1/2 md:w-[400px] z-[1000] transition-transform duration-500 ease-out
FloatingActionCard.instructions: Used for 'Report Incident' prompt and 'Live Tracking' info. Google Maps-style clean white surface.

ReportButton.classes: w-full bg-[#EF4444] hover:bg-[#DC2626] text-white rounded-xl py-4 font-bold text-lg flex items-center justify-center gap-3 shadow-[0_8px_16px_-6px_rgba(239,68,68,0.5)] transition-all active:scale-[0.98]
ReportButton.instructions: Must feel massive and clickable. Red signifies emergency/incident reporting.

elements: Header: 'Officer En Route' with pulsing green dot.
Metrics Row: ETA (large, bold tabular-nums) and Distance.
Officer Profile: Small avatar with name 'Officer Rajesh' and badge.

animations.officer_movement: Implement smooth linear interpolation (LERP) for marker movement between coordinate updates. Calculate step delta and update marker position iteratively over time.
animations.ui_mount: Cards must slide up from the bottom on mount, creating a fluid, app-like feel.
animations.pulse: Use Tailwind animate-pulse or ping for critical status indicators.

images.description: Police officer in uniform with sunglasses

media.icons: Use lucide-react. Required: MapPin, Siren/Flame, User, Navigation, CheckCircle2.

testing_attributes.instructions: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Initialize full-screen Leaflet map centered on Anantapur, AP (14.6819, 77.6006).
2. Implement click-to-place-incident functionality. Show bottom FloatingActionCard to confirm report.
3. On 'Report Incident' click: simulate assignment. Find/create a dummy officer marker (Green), turn it Red (Busy), and start movement.
4. For movement: calculate a direct line or polyline to the incident. Use requestAnimationFrame or setInterval to incrementally update the officer's LatLng (smooth LERP).
5. Continuously calculate remaining distance via map.distance(). Display live ETA and Distance in the FloatingActionCard.
6. Draw a Polyline from officer to incident. Trim/update it as the officer moves.
7. When distance < 20m, resolve incident, reset state, show success toast.
8. Strictly follow the Google Maps premium styling: rounded-3xl cards, subtle border, deep shadows, no clutter. UI should make the user want to select it instantly.
